analyst_observations: The skill's biggest win is on fidelity and discipline assertions (evals 1-2). It preserved user quotes, proposed exact replacement text, and avoided inventing extra findings.

Eval 3 is the shared failure. Both variants over-decompose a single user observation into 4-5 technical sub-findings. The skill's 'no scope creep' instruction isn't strong enough to prevent code-archaeology inflation.
The baseline never produced [CLARIFY] tags. The skill did in all 3 runs. This is the unique structural advantage — flagging ambiguity for user confirmation rather than guessing.
Token cost is ~10% higher with the skill. Time cost is ~43% higher, driven primarily by Eval 3 (322s vs 139s). Single-issue feedback should be the cheapest case, not the most expensive.
Non-discriminating assertion: A2 (code trace present) passed for both variants in all evals. Code tracing is a commodity capability — the skill doesn't improve it.
